metadata.name: HIPAA Security & Privacy Rule
metadata.description: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Security Rule establishes national standards to protect individuals' electronic personal health information (ePHI) that is created, received, used, or maintained by a covered entity. The Privacy Rule establishes national standards for the protection of individually identifiable health information. Together, they require appropriate administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI.
metadata.source_organization: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS)
metadata.classification_reasoning: HIPAA is a U.S. federal law enforced by the HHS Office for Civil Rights (OCR). Compliance is mandatory for covered entities and business associates, with civil and criminal penalties for violations.
metadata.framework_purpose: To protect the privacy and security of individually identifiable health information (PHI/ePHI), ensure individuals' rights regarding their health information, and establish standards for electronic health care transactions.
metadata.framework_scope: Covered entities (health plans, health care clearinghouses, health care providers conducting electronic transactions) and their business associates that create, receive, maintain, or transmit protected health information.
framework_objectives: Ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all ePHI
Protect against reasonably anticipated threats or hazards to the security of ePHI
Protect against impermissible uses or disclosures of ePHI
Ensure workforce compliance with security and privacy requirements
Establish individuals' rights over their health information
metadata.target_audience: Health Plans, Health Care Providers, Health Care Clearinghouses, Business Associates, Privacy Officers, Security Officers, Compliance Teams, IT Security Teams
metadata.regulatory_authority: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights (OCR)
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Civil monetary penalties range from $100 to $50,000 per violation (up to $1.5 million per year for identical violations). Criminal penalties can include fines up to $250,000 and imprisonment up to 10 years. OCR may also require corrective action plans and monitoring.
adoption_approach: Designate a Security Officer and Privacy Officer
Conduct a comprehensive risk analysis of ePHI
Develop and implement required policies and procedures
Execute Business Associate Agreements with all applicable parties
Implement administrative, physical, and technical safeguards
Conduct workforce training on security and privacy requirements
Perform periodic evaluations and risk assessments

controls.title: Security Management Process - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.
controls.full_text: A covered entity or business associate must implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. This is the overarching standard for the security management process, requiring a comprehensive approach to managing ePHI security risks.

controls.title: Risk Analysis
controls.description: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of ePHI held by the covered entity or business associate.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information held by the covered entity or business associate. The risk analysis must be comprehensive, covering all ePHI regardless of the medium or location, and must be updated periodically.
evidence_requirements: Risk analysis report documenting all ePHI systems
Asset inventory of systems containing ePHI

controls.title: Risk Management
controls.description: Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with the Security Rule.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Implement security measures sufficient to reduce risks and vulnerabilities to a reasonable and appropriate level to comply with §164.306(a). The risk management process must address the risks identified in the risk analysis, and security measures must be documented and implemented.
evidence_requirements: Risk management plan with mitigation strategies

controls.title: Sanction Policy
controls.description: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate. The sanction policy must be documented and communicated to all workforce members.

controls.title: Information System Activity Review
controls.description: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports. Reviews must be conducted on a regular basis to detect potential security incidents and ensure ongoing compliance.
evidence_requirements: Audit log review procedures and schedule

controls.title: Assigned Security Responsibility
controls.description: Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by the Security Rule.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by this subpart for the covered entity or business associate. This individual serves as the HIPAA Security Officer and must have appropriate authority and resources.
evidence_requirements: Security Officer job description and responsibilities
Evidence of authority and resource allocation

controls.title: Workforce Security - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of the workforce have appropriate access to ePHI and to prevent unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures to ensure that all members of its workforce have appropriate access to electronic protected health information, as provided under the information access management standard, and to prevent those workforce members who do not have access from obtaining access to ePHI.

controls.title: Authorization and/or Supervision
controls.description: Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with ePHI or in locations where it might be accessed.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement procedures for the authorization and/or supervision of workforce members who work with electronic protected health information or in locations where it might be accessed. Authorization procedures must ensure that access is granted based on job function and need.

controls.title: Workforce Clearance Procedure
controls.description: Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to ePHI is appropriate.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement procedures to determine that the access of a workforce member to electronic protected health information is appropriate. This includes background checks, verification of qualifications, and determination of appropriate access levels.

controls.title: Termination Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for terminating access to ePHI when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement procedures for terminating access to electronic protected health information when the employment of, or other arrangement with, a workforce member ends or as required by determinations made under the workforce clearance procedure. This includes revoking access, retrieving keys and badges, and changing passwords.

controls.title: Information Access Management - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to ePHI that are consistent with the applicable requirements of the Privacy Rule.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part (the Privacy Rule). Access management must ensure that ePHI is accessible only to authorized individuals based on their role and need.

controls.title: Isolating Health Care Clearinghouse Functions
controls.description: If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, the clearinghouse must implement policies and procedures that protect ePHI from unauthorized access by the larger organization.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification (for clearinghouses). If a health care clearinghouse is part of a larger organization, the clearinghouse must implement policies and procedures that protect the electronic protected health information of the clearinghouse from unauthorized access by the larger organization.
evidence_requirements: Access control evidence for clearinghouse functions

controls.title: Access Authorization
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures for granting access to ePHI, for example, through access to a workstation, transaction, program, process, or other mechanism.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement policies and procedures for granting access to electronic protected health information, for example, through access to a workstation, transaction, program, process, or other mechanism. Access authorization must be based on the principle of minimum necessary.

controls.title: Access Establishment and Modification
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the entity's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement policies and procedures that, based upon the covered entity's or business associate's access authorization policies, establish, document, review, and modify a user's right of access to a workstation, transaction, program, or process.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training - Standard
controls.description: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of the workforce, including management.
controls.full_text: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of its workforce (including management). The program must address the security policies and procedures of the entity and be provided to all new workforce members and periodically thereafter.
evidence_requirements: Training completion records for all workforce members

controls.title: Security Reminders
controls.description: Periodic security updates provided to all workforce members.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Periodic security updates. Provide periodic security reminders to workforce members covering topics such as password management, malware protection, and social engineering awareness.

controls.title: Protection from Malicious Software
controls.description: Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Procedures for guarding against, detecting, and reporting malicious software. Training must include awareness of malware threats, procedures for recognizing suspicious activity, and reporting requirements.

controls.title: Log-in Monitoring
controls.description: Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Procedures for monitoring log-in attempts and reporting discrepancies. Organizations should implement procedures to monitor login activity, detect unauthorized access attempts, and report suspicious login behavior.

controls.title: Password Management
controls.description: Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Procedures for creating, changing, and safeguarding passwords. The organization should establish and communicate password complexity requirements, change frequency, and secure handling procedures.

controls.title: Security Incident Procedures - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents. The entity must have a comprehensive incident management process for identifying, responding to, mitigating, and documenting security incidents involving ePHI.

controls.title: Response and Reporting
controls.description: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known to the entity; and document security incidents and their outcomes.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known to the covered entity or business associate; and document security incidents and their outcomes. Incident response procedures must be tested and refined based on lessons learned.

controls.title: Contingency Plan - Standard
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence that damages systems that contain ePHI.
controls.full_text: Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence (for example, fire, vandalism, system failure, and natural disaster) that damages systems that contain electronic protected health information.

controls.title: Data Backup Plan
controls.description: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of ePHI.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of electronic protected health information. Backups must be performed on a regular schedule, stored securely, and tested for recoverability.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery Plan
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data. The disaster recovery plan must identify critical systems and data, define recovery procedures, establish recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO), and be tested periodically.

controls.title: Emergency Mode Operation Plan
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of ePHI while operating in emergency mode.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to enable continuation of critical business processes for protection of the security of electronic protected health information while operating in emergency mode. The plan must identify essential functions and the mechanisms to support them during an emergency.

controls.title: Testing and Revision Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement procedures for periodic testing and revision of contingency plans. Testing should validate that backup data can be restored, that disaster recovery procedures function as expected, and that emergency mode operations can be sustained.
evidence_requirements: Plan revision records based on test outcomes

controls.title: Applications and Data Criticality Analysis
controls.description: Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Assess the relative criticality of specific applications and data in support of other contingency plan components. The analysis should classify applications and data by their importance to operations and patient care to prioritize recovery efforts.

controls.title: Evaluation
controls.description: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and subsequently in response to environmental or operational changes.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and, subsequently, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of electronic protected health information, that establishes the extent to which a covered entity's or business associate's security policies and procedures meet the requirements of this subpart.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts and Other Arrangements
controls.description: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI on its behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances through a written contract or arrangement.
controls.full_text: A covered entity may permit a business associate to create, receive, maintain, or transmit electronic protected health information on the covered entity's behalf only if the covered entity obtains satisfactory assurances, in accordance with §164.314(a), that the business associate will appropriately safeguard the information. A covered entity is not required to obtain such satisfactory assurances from a business associate that is a subcontractor.

controls.title: Written Contract or Other Arrangement
controls.description: Document the satisfactory assurances required through a written contract or other arrangement with the business associate.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Document the satisfactory assurances required by §164.308(b)(1) through a written contract or other arrangement with the business associate that meets the applicable requirements of §164.314(a). The contract must specify the permitted and required uses of ePHI, require safeguards, and mandate breach reporting.

controls.title: Facility Access Controls - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to its electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed. Physical safeguards must protect the physical infrastructure housing ePHI systems.

controls.title: Contingency Operations
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Establish (and implement as needed) procedures that allow facility access in support of restoration of lost data under the disaster recovery plan and emergency mode operations plan in the event of an emergency.

controls.title: Facility Security Plan
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement policies and procedures to safeguard the facility and the equipment therein from unauthorized physical access, tampering, and theft. The facility security plan must address perimeter security, access controls, surveillance, and visitor management.

controls.title: Access Control and Validation Procedures
controls.description: Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement procedures to control and validate a person's access to facilities based on their role or function, including visitor control and control of access to software programs for testing and revision.

controls.title: Maintenance Records
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement policies and procedures to document repairs and modifications to the physical components of a facility which are related to security (for example, hardware, walls, doors, and locks).

controls.title: Workstation Use
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access ePHI.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Implement policies and procedures that specify the proper functions to be performed, the manner in which those functions are to be performed, and the physical attributes of the surroundings of a specific workstation or class of workstation that can access electronic protected health information.
evidence_requirements: Workstation placement and screen privacy requirements

controls.title: Workstation Security
controls.description: Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access ePHI, to restrict access to authorized users.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Implement physical safeguards for all workstations that access electronic protected health information, to restrict access to authorized users. This includes physical placement, cable locks, secure areas, and screen privacy filters.

controls.title: Device and Media Controls - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain ePHI into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain electronic protected health information into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

controls.title: Disposal
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of ePHI, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of electronic protected health information, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored. Disposal methods must render ePHI unreadable, indecipherable, and otherwise unable to be reconstructed.

controls.title: Media Re-use
controls.description: Implement procedures for removal of ePHI from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Implement procedures for removal of electronic protected health information from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use. Sanitization procedures must ensure complete removal of ePHI from all media being reused.

controls.title: Accountability
controls.description: Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Maintain a record of the movements of hardware and electronic media and any person responsible therefore. An accountability system must track who has custody of devices and media containing ePHI.

controls.title: Data Backup and Storage
controls.description: Create a retrievable, exact copy of ePHI, when needed, before movement of equipment.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Create a retrievable, exact copy of electronic protected health information, when needed, before movement of equipment. This ensures ePHI availability is maintained during hardware relocations or decommissioning.

controls.title: Access Control - Standard
controls.description: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain ePHI to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.
controls.full_text: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in §164.308(a)(4).

controls.title: Unique User Identification
controls.description: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity. Each user must have a unique identifier to enable individual accountability and auditing of system access.

controls.title: Emergency Access Procedure
controls.description: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary ePHI during an emergency.
controls.full_text: Required implementation specification. Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary electronic protected health information during an emergency. Emergency access procedures must balance the need for immediate access with maintaining security controls.

controls.title: Automatic Logoff
controls.description: Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement electronic procedures that terminate an electronic session after a predetermined time of inactivity. Session timeout settings must be configured on all systems that access ePHI to prevent unauthorized access from unattended workstations.
evidence_requirements: System configuration screenshots showing timeout settings
Group policy or MDM configuration evidence

controls.title: Encryption and Decryption
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt ePHI.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement a mechanism to encrypt and decrypt electronic protected health information. Where encryption is determined to be reasonable and appropriate, data at rest must be encrypted using validated encryption algorithms.
evidence_requirements: Encryption policy for data at rest
Encryption algorithm and key length documentation

controls.title: Audit Controls
controls.description: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use ePHI.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information. Audit controls must capture sufficient detail to support security incident investigation and compliance monitoring.
evidence_requirements: Audit log samples from ePHI systems
Audit log review procedures and reports

controls.title: Integrity - Standard
controls.description: Implement policies and procedures to protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction.
controls.full_text: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction. Data integrity controls must ensure that ePHI is not modified or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.
evidence_requirements: Change control documentation for ePHI systems

controls.title: Mechanism to Authenticate Electronic Protected Health Information
controls.description: Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement electronic mechanisms to corroborate that electronic protected health information has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner. Mechanisms may include checksums, hash functions, digital signatures, or error-correcting codes.

controls.title: Person or Entity Authentication
controls.description: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to ePHI is the one claimed.
controls.full_text: Required standard. Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed. Authentication mechanisms must provide reasonable assurance of identity before granting access to ePHI systems.

controls.title: Transmission Security - Standard
controls.description: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to ePHI that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
controls.full_text: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

controls.title: Integrity Controls
controls.description: Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement security measures to ensure that electronically transmitted electronic protected health information is not improperly modified without detection until disposed of. This may include message authentication codes, digital signatures, or TLS with integrity checking.

controls.title: Encryption
controls.description: Implement a mechanism to encrypt ePHI whenever deemed appropriate during transmission.
controls.full_text: Addressable implementation specification. Implement a mechanism to encrypt electronic protected health information whenever deemed appropriate. Encryption of ePHI in transit is strongly recommended using TLS 1.2 or higher, VPN tunnels, or equivalent encryption mechanisms.

controls.title: Business Associate Contracts or Other Arrangements - Standard
controls.description: The contract or other arrangement between the covered entity and its business associate must meet the requirements of §164.314(a)(2).
controls.full_text: The contract or other arrangement between the covered entity and its business associate required by §164.308(b)(3) must meet the requirements of §164.314(a)(2)(i) or (a)(2)(ii), as applicable. Business associate agreements must contain specific provisions ensuring the security of ePHI.
evidence_requirements: Executed BAAs with all business associates

controls.title: Requirements for Group Health Plans
controls.description: A group health plan must ensure that its plan documents provide that the plan sponsor will reasonably and appropriately safeguard ePHI.
controls.full_text: Except when the only electronic protected health information disclosed to a plan sponsor is disclosed pursuant to §164.504(f)(1)(ii) or (iii), or as authorized under §164.508, a group health plan must ensure that its plan documents provide that the plan sponsor will reasonably and appropriately safeguard electronic protected health information created, received, maintained, or transmitted to or by the plan sponsor on behalf of the group health plan.
evidence_requirements: Plan document amendments for ePHI safeguards
Group health plan ePHI handling procedures

controls.title: Notice of Privacy Practices
controls.description: A covered entity must provide a notice of its privacy practices to individuals and make the notice available to anyone who asks for it.
controls.full_text: A covered entity must provide a notice of its privacy practices for protected health information. The notice must describe the uses and disclosures of PHI that may be made, the individual's rights, and the covered entity's legal duties. It must be provided to individuals at or before the first service delivery and be posted prominently at the facility.
evidence_requirements: Current Notice of Privacy Practices document

controls.title: Rights to Request Privacy Protection for PHI
controls.description: Individuals have the right to request restrictions on certain uses and disclosures of PHI and to request confidential communications.
controls.full_text: A covered entity must permit an individual to request that the covered entity restrict uses or disclosures of PHI for treatment, payment, or health care operations, and disclosures to persons involved in the individual's care. The entity must also accommodate reasonable requests for confidential communications. The covered entity is not required to agree to a restriction except when requested to restrict disclosures to a health plan for services paid in full out of pocket.

controls.title: Access of Individuals to Protected Health Information
controls.description: Individuals have the right to inspect and obtain a copy of their PHI in designated record sets, with limited exceptions.
controls.full_text: An individual has a right of access to inspect and obtain a copy of protected health information about the individual in a designated record set, for as long as the protected health information is maintained in the designated record set. The covered entity must act on a request within 30 days (with a possible 30-day extension) and must provide the information in the form and format requested if readily producible.
evidence_requirements: Access request tracking log with response timelines

controls.title: Amendment of Protected Health Information
controls.description: Individuals have the right to request amendment of their PHI in designated record sets.
controls.full_text: An individual has the right to have a covered entity amend protected health information or a record about the individual in a designated record set for as long as the protected health information is maintained in the designated record set. The covered entity must act on an amendment request within 60 days and must inform the individual of the acceptance or denial, with the right to submit a statement of disagreement.
evidence_requirements: Denial and statement of disagreement records

controls.title: Accounting of Disclosures of Protected Health Information
controls.description: Individuals have the right to receive an accounting of disclosures of PHI made by the covered entity in the six years prior to the request.
controls.full_text: An individual has a right to receive an accounting of disclosures of protected health information made by a covered entity in the six years prior to the date on which the accounting is requested, except for disclosures for treatment, payment, and health care operations; disclosures to the individual; and other exceptions. The covered entity must act on the request within 60 days.

controls.title: Minimum Necessary Standard
controls.description: When using or disclosing PHI or when requesting PHI from another covered entity or business associate, a covered entity must make reasonable efforts to limit PHI to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose.
controls.full_text: When using or disclosing protected health information or when requesting protected health information from another covered entity or business associate, a covered entity or business associate must make reasonable efforts to limit protected health information to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose of the use, disclosure, or request. This does not apply to disclosures to the individual, disclosures pursuant to authorization, or disclosures required by law.
evidence_requirements: Role-based access definitions limiting PHI access
Minimum necessary determinations for routine disclosures
Staff training on minimum necessary standard

controls.title: Uses and Disclosures for Which an Authorization Is Required
controls.description: A covered entity must obtain a valid authorization for uses and disclosures of PHI not otherwise permitted or required by the Privacy Rule.
controls.full_text: Except as otherwise permitted or required by the Privacy Rule, a covered entity may not use or disclose protected health information without an authorization that is valid under this section. Valid authorizations must contain specific core elements including description of information, persons authorized, purpose, expiration date, and individual's signature. Special provisions apply to psychotherapy notes, marketing, and sale of PHI.
evidence_requirements: Authorization form templates with required elements

controls.title: De-identification of Protected Health Information
controls.description: Health information that does not identify an individual and with respect to which there is no reasonable basis to believe the information can be used to identify an individual is not individually identifiable health information.
controls.full_text: A covered entity may determine that health information is not individually identifiable health information only if the covered entity has no actual knowledge that the information could be used alone or in combination with other information to identify an individual. Two methods are available: Expert Determination (applying statistical and scientific principles) or Safe Harbor (removing 18 specified identifiers).
evidence_requirements: Expert determination or safe harbor method documentation

controls.title: Privacy Officer Designation
controls.description: A covered entity must designate a privacy official responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures of the entity.
controls.full_text: A covered entity must designate a privacy official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures of the entity required by the Privacy Rule. The covered entity must also designate a contact person or office responsible for receiving complaints about the entity's compliance with the Privacy Rule.

controls.title: Privacy Training
controls.description: A covered entity must train all members of its workforce on the policies and procedures with respect to PHI, as necessary and appropriate for them to carry out their functions.
controls.full_text: A covered entity must train all members of its workforce on the policies and procedures with respect to protected health information required by the Privacy Rule, as necessary and appropriate for the members of the workforce to carry out their functions. Training must be provided to each new member of the workforce within a reasonable period after joining and when material changes to policies or procedures occur.
evidence_requirements: Training completion records for all workforce members

controls.title: Breach Notification Rule
controls.description: Following a breach of unsecured PHI, covered entities must provide notification of the breach to affected individuals, the Secretary, and, in certain circumstances, to the media.
controls.full_text: A covered entity must notify affected individuals, the HHS Secretary, and in some cases the media, following the discovery of a breach of unsecured protected health information. Individual notices must be provided without unreasonable delay and in no case later than 60 calendar days from the discovery. Business associates must notify the covered entity of breaches. Notifications must include specific content including a description of the breach, types of information involved, steps individuals should take, and what the entity is doing to mitigate harm.
